title: VSA Foundations
desc: Build the language of Volume Spread Analysis: volume, spread, close, background, supply, demand, effort and result.
learningOutcomes: Explain the central VSA problem addressed in vsa foundations.
Read volume, spread, close and background together rather than as isolated signals.
Describe confirmation and invalidation before forming a trade idea.
Convert the concept into a practice task or review question.
assessment: Mini quiz, chart-reading drill, and journal reflection
lessons: What Volume Spread Analysis Really Studies
Why Volume Matters More Than Candle Colour
Supply and Demand as the Core Story
Tick Volume in Spot Forex: Honest Use and Limits
Spread as the Visible Result of Effort
Closing Position: Where the Battle Ended
Background First: Why One Candle Is Never Enough
Effort versus Result: The Master Principle

title: Candle Anatomy in VSA
desc: Learn to read individual bars through direction, range, volume, close and the next bar response.
learningOutcomes: Explain the central VSA problem addressed in candle anatomy in vsa.
Read volume, spread, close and background together rather than as isolated signals.
Describe confirmation and invalidation before forming a trade idea.
Convert the concept into a practice task or review question.
assessment: Mini quiz, chart-reading drill, and journal reflection
lessons: Up Bars and Down Bars in VSA
Wide Spread Bars: Progress or Exhaustion?
Narrow Spread Bars: Lack of Result
High Volume Bars: Activity, Not Always Strength
Low Volume Bars: Interest Has Dried Up
Ultra High Volume Bars and Climactic Behaviour
Close on the High: When Demand Controls the Bar
Close in the Middle: Conflict Inside the Bar
Close on the Low: When Supply Controls the Bar

title: Volume Interpretation
desc: Study volume as relative activity and learn why location, trend and result decide its meaning.
learningOutcomes: Explain the central VSA problem addressed in volume interpretation.
Read volume, spread, close and background together rather than as isolated signals.
Describe confirmation and invalidation before forming a trade idea.
Convert the concept into a practice task or review question.
assessment: Mini quiz, chart-reading drill, and journal reflection
lessons: Relative Volume: Compare With the Recent Past
Volume Spike at a Key Level
Climactic Volume After a Long Move
News Volume and Why It Can Mislead

title: Spread and Close Interpretation
desc: Connect range and closing position to volume so the learner can detect acceptance, rejection, absorption and failure.
learningOutcomes: Explain the central VSA problem addressed in spread and close interpretation.
Read volume, spread, close and background together rather than as isolated signals.
Describe confirmation and invalidation before forming a trade idea.
Convert the concept into a practice task or review question.
assessment: Mini quiz, chart-reading drill, and journal reflection

title: Background Reading
desc: Teach the central VSA habit: judge the current bar only after reading previous price-volume behaviour.
learningOutcomes: Explain the central VSA problem addressed in background reading.
Read volume, spread, close and background together rather than as isolated signals.
Describe confirmation and invalidation before forming a trade idea.
Convert the concept into a practice task or review question.
assessment: Mini quiz, chart-reading drill, and journal reflection

title: Signs of Weakness

Read volume, spread, close and background together rather than as isolated signals.
Describe confirmation and invalidation before forming a trade idea.
Convert the concept into a practice task or review question.

lessons: Effort to Rise with Poor Result

title: Signs of Strength
desc: Recognize demand, selling absorption, lack of supply and strength after false weakness.

title: Tests and Confirmation
